description_long: This is a military-style twin-engine airplane with a dark olive-green fuselage and straight, mid-mounted wings. The wings are broad and feature red star insignias, suggesting a Soviet or Russian origin. The aircraft has two prominent propeller engines mounted on the wings, each with yellow-tipped black blades. The tail section features a twin vertical stabilizer (H-tail) configuration, and the fuselage is streamlined with a glazed, forward-mounted cockpit. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable. The surface appears metallic with a matte finish, and the airplane is detailed with panel lines and insignia.
description: A military-style, twin-propeller airplane with olive-green coloring, red star insignias, and a twin-tail design. It features a streamlined fuselage, mid-mounted straight wings, and a forward, enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin propeller engines, broad straight wings, and forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the twin vertical stabilizers, wing insignia, and streamlined fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the H-tail configuration and the full wingspan with visible engine nacelles.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the propeller, cockpit, and tail structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings with visible propellers.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a horizontal stabilizer connecting them.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-panel canopy.
